title: Hot Honey Chicken with Roasted Potatoes & Pickled Jalapeños
description: Blue Apron fan favorite — crispy pan-seared chicken thighs glazed in a fiery hot honey sauce, served with golden roasted potatoes and tangy pickled jalapeños. The sweet heat combination is absolutely irresistible.
instructions: Preheat oven to 425°F (220°C). Cut potatoes into 1-inch cubes. Toss with 1½ tbsp olive oil, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper. Roast on a baking sheet for 25–30 minutes, flipping halfway, until golden and crispy.
Make pickled jalapeños: thinly slice jalapeños and toss with apple cider vinegar, a pinch of sugar, and a pinch of salt. Let sit while you cook.
Make hot honey: whisk together honey, hot sauce, and minced garlic. Set aside.
Pat chicken thighs dry. Season with salt and pepper.
Heat 1½ tbsp olive oil in an oven-safe skillet over medium-high heat. Add chicken skin-side down; cook 5–6 minutes until skin is golden and crispy. Flip and cook 2 more minutes.
Transfer skillet to oven and roast 15–18 minutes until chicken reaches 165°F.
Remove chicken from oven. Spoon off excess fat. Return skillet to medium heat. Add butter and thyme; let butter foam. Spoon hot honey over chicken and cook 1–2 minutes until glazed.
Serve chicken over or alongside roasted potatoes. Top with pickled jalapeños and a drizzle of any remaining hot honey from the pan.
tips: For extra crispy skin, pat the chicken very dry and let it sit uncovered in the fridge for 30 minutes before cooking.
The pickled jalapeños balance the sweetness of the honey perfectly — they're worth the 5 minutes of effort.
Adjust the heat level by using more or less hot sauce in the honey.
This hot honey glaze is also excellent on salmon, pork chops, or roasted vegetables.
